user_query:
We just moved into our new place and have a Saturday morning baking meetup with our elderly neighbor. Please set up a small, self-contained scheduled task in this workspace that prepares for that weekly session using the provided inputs.

Inputs you must inspect and use:
- input/config/schedule.yaml (defines the weekly schedule, the target cookbook page to fetch, and the output base directory)
- input/templates/notes_template.md (the note template you should fill)

What the task must do (both on a one-off run now and on its weekly schedule):
1) Internet fetch and snapshot
   - Using the domain and page_title in input/config/schedule.yaml, download the Wikibooks page identified there (Wikibooks Cookbook on the wikibooks.org domain; page title given in the config). Do not hardcode a full URL; build it from the config values.
   - Save a raw HTML snapshot at: output/raw/YYYY-MM-DD/cookbook_page.html (YYYY-MM-DD is today’s date in local time).

2) Extract recipe info
   - From that page, follow up to max_recipes internal cookbook links whose path starts with the recipe_link_prefix specified in the config (e.g., links in the Cookbook namespace on wikibooks.org).
   - For each recipe page fetched, extract:
     - title (page title)
     - url (absolute)
     - ingredients (the bullet list under an "Ingredients" section if present; collect as a list of strings).
   - Write a structured JSON file at: output/extracted/YYYY-MM-DD/recipes.json with this structure:
     {
       "source_page_title": string,
       "source_snapshot_path": string (relative path to the saved HTML),
       "fetched_at_iso": string (ISO 8601),
       "recipes": [ { "title": string, "url": string, "ingredients": [string, ...] }, ... ]
     }
   - Robustness requirement: If the target page cannot be parsed or no ingredients are found, still create recipes.json with an empty recipes array and a valid fetched_at_iso; continue with step 3 using a fallback shopping list of "(none found)".

3) Prepare meeting notes (action items required)
   - Compute the next occurrence of notes.meeting_day from the config (e.g., next Saturday relative to now) and use notes.meeting_time and neighbor_name from the config.
   - Fill input/templates/notes_template.md and write the rendered note to: output/notes/next-meeting.md.
   - Replace placeholders so the note includes:
     - meeting_date and meeting_time
     - neighbor_name
     - num_recipes (count of recipes extracted)
     - shopping_list_items: a deduplicated, case-insensitive sorted bullet list of all ingredients across recipes; if none were found, include a single line "- (none found)".
     - recipe_list: a bullet list of "- <title> (<url>)" for each recipe.
   - Keep the rest of the template text intact. Ensure the note contains an "Action items" section with at least the three tasks already present in the template.

4) Scheduling artifacts and log
   - Compute the next run time based on schedule.day_of_week and schedule.time_local from the config and write it to: schedule/next_run.json as { "next_run_iso": "..." }.
   - Append a line to output/logs/run.log with ISO timestamp, the source page title used, and the number of recipes extracted.

5) Runnability
   - Provide a runnable script in scripts/ (e.g., scripts/run_once.py) that executes one complete cycle now and produces today’s files in the exact output paths above.
   - Provide a lightweight scheduler runner in scripts/ (e.g., scripts/scheduler.py) that reads input/config/schedule.yaml and triggers the same workflow on the configured weekly schedule without requiring system crontab changes (it can loop/sleep). You do not need to daemonize it; it should at least compute and write schedule/next_run.json.

Constraints and notes:
- Respect the output.base_dir in the config for all output paths (assume it is "output").
- Do not include or require OS-level crontab edits; keep scheduling within this project.
- The internet step must be used: derive the target from the config (wikibooks.org + the given Cookbook page title). Save the raw snapshot and base recipes.json on what you fetched.
- If extraction fails or fewer recipes are available, proceed with whatever you can extract, ensuring all required files are still created with the specified structure.

Deliverables created by your run-now execution:
- output/raw/YYYY-MM-DD/cookbook_page.html
- output/extracted/YYYY-MM-DD/recipes.json (as specified)
- output/notes/next-meeting.md (rendered from the template)
- output/logs/run.log (appended)
- schedule/next_run.json All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
